items.q: What is the key structural difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
items.correct: Eukaryotes have a membrane-bound nucleus and membrane-bound organelles; prokaryotes do not.
items.misconception: Prokaryotes have no DNA — that's why they have no nucleus.
items.random_wrong: Prokaryotes are always smaller than 1 micrometre and have no cytoplasm.
items.why: Students see 'no nucleus' and think 'no DNA', missing that prokaryotes still have DNA — it's just free in the cytoplasm.

items.correct: Yes — plants respire all the time to release energy; they also photosynthesise when there is light.
items.misconception: Plants only photosynthesise and do not respire; respiration is for animals only.
items.random_wrong: Plants only respire at night and only photosynthesise during the day; the processes never overlap.
items.why: Because photosynthesis is taught as the plant equivalent of respiration, students think it replaces respiration in plants entirely.

items.q: How do vaccines protect you from a disease?
items.correct: They expose your immune system to a harmless form of the pathogen so memory cells form, allowing rapid response if exposed later.
items.misconception: Vaccines kill the pathogen if it ever enters the body.
items.random_wrong: Vaccines provide antibodies for life by adding them directly to the blood.
items.why: Students think vaccines actively fight pathogens themselves, rather than priming the immune system to fight.

items.q: What is a hybridoma made by fusing?
items.correct: A B cell and a tumour cell
items.why: Correct answer: A B cell and a tumour cell. “Two B lymphocytes” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which drug was originally discovered in willow bark?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Aspirin. “Penicillin” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A monoclonal antibody pregnancy test detects hCG. If the urine contains 25 hCG molecules per microlitre, how many are in a 4 microlitre sample?
items.why: Exact answer: 100. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
